headline: Malacca Coastal Reclamation Alters 22km of Natural Shoreline
context: Malacca's 3 major reclamation projects — Melaka Gateway, Pulau Melaka, and Melaka Waterfront — have collectively altered 22km of natural shoreline since 2014.

cards.big: Malacca's Straits are one of the world's busiest shipping lanes.
cards.sub: 22km of new artificial shoreline has altered currents, increasing sedimentation at the UNESCO World Heritage port.

cards.big: Fishermen at Kampung Tanjung Kling report 45% catch decline since 2018.
cards.sub: Turbidity from reclamation destroyed seagrass beds that served as fish nursery habitat.

cards.big: Melaka Gateway: RM43B project, now 8% complete after 10 years.
cards.sub: The developer entered receivership in 2023. Environmental damage from partial reclamation is irreversible.

cards.big: RAMSAR-listed wetlands at Sungai Pulai received no buffer from reclamation.
cards.sub: International treaty obligations were not incorporated into the state approval process.

cards.big: A RM43B project that is 8% built and 100% damaging is not development. It is a wound.
Malacca's reclamation approvals prioritised speculative investment over environmental carrying capacity. The lesson is that mega-projects need completion guarantees, not just launch ceremonies.
